req.title: Accessibility compliance
req.statement: All UI screens shall be keyboard-navigable and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-112 mandates WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility across all HR-app screens. Three orthogonal test cases cover: (1) EP-positive – a representative compliant screen passes axe contrast and keyboard-nav checks; (2) NEG-negative – a known non-compliant page (injected low-contrast element) is detected and violations are reported; (3) BVA-boundary – the exact AA contrast threshold (4.5:1 for normal text) is exercised by injecting an element whose computed ratio sits precisely at the boundary value, confirming pass/fail behaviour at the limit. Each test uses Playwright via pytest-playwright, axe-playwright for automated a11y scanning, and custom <helper_fn> placeholders for keyboard-path traversal and ratio injection.

tests.name: test_compliant_screen_passes_wcag_aa
preconditions: hr-app is deployed and reachable at BASE_URL
Page at /dashboard renders without JS errors
axe-core is available via pytest-axe or axe-playwright
actions: Wait for page to reach network-idle state
Run axe accessibility scan scoped to WCAG 2.1 AA rules
Simulate Tab-key traversal through all focusable elements
expected: axe scan returns zero violations with impact critical or serious
Every interactive element receives visible focus during Tab traversal
No element has contrast ratio below 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page
from axe_playwright_python.sync_playwright import Axe

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:3000"

@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
def page(browser):
    ctx = browser.new_context()
    pg = ctx.new_page()
    yield pg
    ctx.close()

def test_compliant_screen_passes_wcag_aa(page: Page):
    page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/dashboard", wait_until="networkidle")
    axe = Axe()
    results = axe.run(page, options={"runOnly": {"type": "tag", "values": ["wcag2aa"]}})
    violations = [v for v in results.violations if v["impact"] in ("critical", "serious")]
    assert violations == [], f"A11y violations: {violations}"
    focusable = page.query_selector_all(
        "a[href], button:not([disabled]), input:not([disabled]), "
        "select:not([disabled]), textarea:not([disabled]), [tabindex='0']"
    )
    assert len(focusable) > 0, "No focusable elements found"
    focused_count = <helper_fn:tab_through_all_focusable>(page, len(focusable))
    assert focused_count == len(focusable), (
        f"Only {focused_count}/{len(focusable)} elements received focus"
    )

tests.name: test_low_contrast_element_triggers_violation
preconditions: hr-app is deployed and reachable at BASE_URL
Test harness can inject inline styles via page.evaluate
actions: Wait for page to reach network-idle state

Inject a visible

element with foreground #aaaaaa on background #ffffff (contrast ~2.3:1, well below AA)
Run axe accessibility scan scoped to WCAG 2.1 AA color-contrast rule
expected: axe scan detects at least one color-contrast violation
Violation references the injected element or its selector
Test asserts violation list is non-empty and maps to rule id 'color-contrast'
trace: R-WEB-112: contrast passes – confirmed by detecting failure when contrast is absent

tests.name: test_contrast_ratio_at_aa_boundary_45_to_1
preconditions: hr-app is deployed and reachable at BASE_URL
Test harness can inject elements and execute JS contrast ratio computation
actions: Wait for page to reach network-idle state

Inject a

element whose foreground/background yields contrast ratio exactly 4.5:1 (e.g. #767676 on #ffffff)
Compute the actual ratio via JS helper and assert it equals 4.5 within tolerance 0.05
Run axe color-contrast rule on the injected element only

Inject a second

element at ratio 4.49:1 and confirm axe flags it as a violation
expected: Element at exactly 4.5:1 passes axe color-contrast rule (AA threshold met)
Element at 4.49:1 fails axe color-contrast rule (just below AA threshold)
Computed JS ratio for 4.5:1 element is within [4.45, 4.55]
trace: R-WEB-112: WCAG 2.1 AA normal text threshold is 4.5:1
R-WEB-112: boundary at exactly AA limit and one step below
